mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above its recent neutral level and CPIAUCSL keeps decelerating, markets infer policy restraint is ending and discount rates will fall; this should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if inflation re-accelerates while rates stay elevated.
failure_condition: If 20d forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3m inflation is falling, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent floor signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which should pressure equity earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with UNRATE up more than 0.3 percentage points in 3 months produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A joint improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broadening final demand and housing activity, which should support cyclical revenue expectations and push S&P 500 higher over the next quarter. The effect is strongest when both series accelerate together rather than separately.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous 3-month gains in RSAFS and HOUST do not correspond to above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive value versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: An upward turn in INDPRO after a prior contraction signals that industrial output is recovering faster than expected, which should improve cyclical earnings revisions and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal fails if industrial production is still falling.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% m/m after at least 2 prior monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if the specified rebound trigger is followed by negative 5d SPX returns in a majority of cases or if the median return is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in FEDFUNDS from a restrictive level signals imminent easing and a lower discount rate for equities, so S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if the cut occurs because macro stress is intensifying and earnings expectations collapse faster than rates fall.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 0.50pp over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns after a 0.50pp two-month Fed funds decline are not positive on average or are below the unconditional baseline in the triggered sample.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises, payroll losses are broad enough to signal earnings deterioration and weaker consumer demand, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following month. Either series alone is less informative than the joint labor-market break.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE does not outperform single-series signals and does not coincide with negative 20d SPX returns in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS reflects broad domestic demand weakening, which should reduce revenue growth expectations and push S&P 500 lower over the next quarter. The mechanism is invalid if GDP softens while retail sales remain firm.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with concurrent negative GDPC1 and weak RSAFS growth fail to produce below-zero 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing downturn, a monthly rebound in INDPRO combined with stable employment indicates output is recovering without a broad labor shock, which should support a positive S&P 500 reaction over five trading days. The signal fails if production rebounds alongside worsening unemployment.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO turns positive m/m while UNRATE is flat or falling
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO reversals with non-rising UNRATE do not lead to positive 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears outside manufacturing recoveries.
